title: astragalus depressus
intro: Astragalus depressus, also known as the depressed milk-vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Astragalus depressus is valued for its adaptogenic properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce stress, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health. Astragalus depressus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used to support immune function, reduce fatigue, and improve respiratory health.
